findings.message: record_raw_path stores the raw full path (including query string) as a metric label key with no sanitization, no distinct-value cap, and no 'other' overflow bucket. Unlike every other label in this class (routes/attribution/referrers are all capped via _bump_capped, statuses/error-types/vital-names are fixed enums), this map grows without bound: one entry per unique URL+query string. This is unbounded/high-cardinality and will blow up Prometheus series counts.
findings.suggestion: Drop the query string and sanitize/route-normalize the path, then funnel through _bump_capped with a MAX cap and an 'other' overflow bucket, e.g. self._bump_capped(self._raw_paths, _san(full_path.split('?', 1)[0]), self.MAX_ROUTES). Or remove record_raw_path entirely and rely on the already-bounded record_page_view route label.

findings.message: app_frontend_raw_path_total emits one time series per raw full path with the unsanitized path value as a label (path="{p}"). The values are never run through _san and the series list is never capped, so the metric family's cardinality is unbounded by URL/query-string variety. Every metric label must be a fixed enum or pass through a distinct-value cap with an 'other' overflow bucket.
findings.suggestion: Render from a bounded, sanitized store (see record_raw_path fix) or remove this counter family; the existing app_frontend_page_views_total route label already provides bounded per-route page-view counts.
